content.full_text:
August 19, 1991

Jay Greenwood
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 G Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Greenwood:

In an effort to assure correct and legal disclosure of potential gifts that may be given to Assemblyman Tom Umberg, I am writing to you today to seek your written advice.

Disneyland, which is located within our Assembly District, has a policy of giving away free park entrance tickets at the request of elected officials who represent the city of Anaheim. When tickets are secured they are left by Disneyland at the park's will call window in the name of the VIP guests and internally credited to the official who requested them.

Because Disneyland's ticket request policy does not carry over to elected officials from other areas around the state, various state legislators have contacted our office in an attempt to have the Assemblyman secure free tickets for them and their guests. Evidently the Assemblymember who previously represented our district did this quite a bit and publicized it around the State Capitol.

My question for you is: If the Assemblyman requests tickets for another elected official and their guests, does the cost of the tickets count as a gift to Assemblyman Umberg for requesting them or to the elected official who uses them? Please let me know.

Thank you for your attention to the request and if you have any further questions on this situation, please do not hesitate to contact me at (714) 537-4477.

Sincerely,

GEORGE T. URCH
Chief of Staff
